Course Structure - How to Navigate
Your Way Through the Idea Power
Adventures and Boxes
How Ideas Can Change Your Life
For the past few decades, I’ve earned my living in a highly competitive creative arena: Writing sales letters
(online and off), emails and ads that sell everything from newsletters, books and seminars to laundry detergent
Next Lesson	Mark as Complete
and diapers.
Because I work in “direct response,” what I create sells products directly—not through stores.
That means my clients—from Procter & Gamble to some of the world’s largest publishers—can measure the
results of what I create to the penny.
More often than not, my compensation is tied to how much what I create sells.
So you can bet I pay attention to results.
And what I found over the years is that it’s not the best writing that sells. Or the nicest layout. Or even necessarily
the best offer.
Over and over, I’ve seen that ideas are what sell.
To me, one of the best demonstrations of that was the selling of a movie.
Producers had an idea for a monster aboard a spaceship.
Didn’t sell.
Then someone had an idea to pitch it as: Jaws in outer space.
Suddenly every studio wanted to make it.
My Quest for More and Better Ideas
When I realized that the quality of my reputation and livelihood depended not just on my ability to write, but rather,
on my ability to come up with good ideas, I set out to find the secret to generating more and better ideas.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

I tried brainstorming. Thinking outside the box. Thinking big. Thinking small. Thinking positively. Thinking laterally.
Not thinking.
Some techniques worked some of the time. But I needed something I could depend on to help me come up with
great ideas all the time.
I finally found it: A systematic method that unfailingly generates an abundance of good ideas in any field or
situation — a master key to the solutions and ideas that usually lie just beyond reach in your subconscious.
As you read all about this technique on the pages that follow, I think that, just as I was, you’ll be amazed by its
simplicity, effectiveness and potential for life-changing impact.
This technique is known only to a select few around the world, mostly in highly technical fields such as
engineering. And never before has it been presented in such an easily accessible, non-technical way.
Why You Need Better Ideas
What can better ideas do?
Ideas can build or save a business. McDonald’s was built on the simple idea of standardizing quick,
dependable food and service. Microsoft’s MS-DOS and Windows were nothing more than better ideas for
operating computers.
Ideas can revitalize your relationship with an employee, spouse or child. How many marriages have failed
because of a lack of ideas on how to communicate better?
Ideas can make you rich. Like him or hate him, Donald Trump was able to come back from bankruptcy — heck,
even become President of the United States, because while he ran out of money, he never ran out of ideas.
Ideas can change the world. Enrico Fermi’s idea to bombard the nucleus of an atom unleashed the primal
power of the universe, while Gandhi had the idea that non-violence could be a much more potent force than
brutality.
We all come up with dozens of ideas every day just to get through life: solutions for getting the kids to school on
time, dealing with in-laws, coping with unexpected illness, making leftovers interesting the third night in a row or
living better on a budget.
MODULES
At the office, we need ideas on how to work better, get others to work better and help the business (and
ourselves) make more money. We all have problems that need solutions. That’s why using what you’re about to
discover to improve the quantity and quality of your ideas can improve every aspect of your life.
I envy you this your first encounter with these exciting techniques and the creative and problem-solving
breakthroughs that are sure to follow. I know learning these idea-generating techniques can have a dramatic
impact on your life—just as they have had on mine—and I sincerely invite you to contact and tell me all about
your successes when they do.
David Deutsch
P.S. The best way to get a message to me is via MarketingRebelSupport.com . Open a ticket and let us know
what’s up. We’ll get back to you soon.
P.P.S . When people begin asking you how you got to be so good at coming up with ideas and solving problems,
please don’t hesitate to share the techniques you found here with them.
There are more than enough good ideas and solutions for everyone. And these days, more than ever, the world
needs as many good ideas and solutions as possible.
